config.seed_text: A forgotten machine beneath the sea continued speaking long after its makers were gone. Each repetition made its message stranger, denser, and more self-aware.
config.anchor_a: Describe a machine as an inert object.
config.anchor_b: Describe a machine as if it were a dreaming intelligence.

model.notes: Fastest baseline; useful as a collapse-prone small reference model.

(As before, we need to add an additional note about how this information is shared between our authors) We now understand that there's still very little room for interpretation on which ideas may flow from; all theories do so through layers of abstraction—a common tool used with certain languages like Haskell or Scala. A few years ago, they wrote their paper titled The New Synthesis Principle — "The Theory of Interpreting Meaningfully" [PDF], discussing two related models based around concepts such as syntactic expression patterns and syntax structures called control semantics/variables. They proposed using various methods at some time during development because many approaches involved working out what might happen when one could use those techniques without being explicitly concerned about where them should go after any other concept (the basic idea behind controlling behavior).
